item.title: 土耳其考虑恢复燃油税缓冲机制，因伊朗战争导致油价飙升
item.description: 据知情人士透露，随着伊朗战争推高原油价格，土耳其正评估是否重启一项旨在缓解消费者油价上涨压力的燃油税机制。【原文：Turkey is assessing whether to revive a fuel tax mechanism aimed at cushioning consumers from rising oil prices as war in Iran drives up crude, according to people familiar with the matter.】

item.title: 欧元债券下跌，交易员权衡欧洲央行加息与能源价格飙升
item.description: 欧洲债券跌势扩大，交易员正评估中东冲突可能长期化的风险。欧洲央行首席经济学家此前警告称，这种局面将引发滞胀。【原文：European bonds extended losses as traders weighed the possibility of a prolonged conflict in the Middle East, a scenario the European Central Bank’s chief economist warned would be stagflationary.】

item.title: 非洲最大食品杂货商Shoprite在市场份额竞争中利润率收窄
item.description: 非洲最大的连锁超市通过压低价格来争取市场份额。【原文：Africa’s largest supermarket chain held prices lower in an attempt to win market share.】

item.title: 海湾动荡助推韩国军工企业股价飙升20%创历史新高
item.description: 韩国营收规模最大的军工企业高管明确阐述了该公司深化中东业务布局的理由：威胁四伏。【原文：A top executive at South Korea’s biggest arms-maker by revenue made a clear case for the company’s deepening foray into the Middle East: Threats abound.】

item.title: 银行呼吁英国国库券豁免杠杆规则
item.description: 银行正敦促英国官员将英国国库券排除在关键监管资本指标之外，以助力该国扩大市场规模。【原文：Banks are asking British officials to exempt the UK’s Treasury bills from a key regulatory capital metric to help the nation expand the market.】

item.title: 埃利奥特将从丰田工业诉讼案中获利5亿美元
item.description: 据彭博社估算，埃利奥特投资管理公司与日本实力雄厚的丰田集团历时数月的博弈，预计将为其带来约5亿美元收益。【原文：Elliott Investment Management is set to make around $500 million from its months-long tussle with Japan’s powerful Toyota group, according to Bloomberg calculations.】

item.title: 能源危机恐致英格兰银行通胀目标再度落空
item.description: 最新分析显示，中东战争引发的能源价格持续飙升，可能导致英国通胀率直至明年都无法降至英国央行设定的2%目标，并推迟降息时机。【原文：A sustained energy price surge caused by war in the Middle East could stop UK inflation falling to the Bank of England’s 2% target until next year and delay interest-rate cuts, according to new analysis.】

item.title: 交易员抛售押注英央行二次降息，通胀担忧持续发酵
item.description: 随着中东冲突持续升级，通胀担忧再度升温，交易员已将英国央行今年二次降息的可能性排除在定价之外。【原文：Traders have priced out the probability of a second interest-rate cut from the Bank of England this year as the deepening conflict in the Middle East rekindles fears of inflation.】

item.title: 欧洲股市暴跌，创四月以来最大两日跌幅
item.description: 欧洲股市正朝着自4月以来最大的两日跌幅迈进，中东冲突加剧推高了能源价格。【原文：European stocks headed for the biggest two-day drop since April as conflict in the Middle East intensified and pushed up energy prices.】

item.title: 日本电力期货跳涨，伊朗冲突威胁天然气供应
item.description: 中东局势升级促使交易员对冲能源成本上涨风险，日本电力期货价格应声跳涨。【原文：Japanese power futures jumped after the escalating conflict in the Middle East spurred traders to hedge against rising energy costs.】

item.title: 维勒罗伊称欧洲央行不会仅依据能源价格设定利率
item.description: 欧洲央行理事会成员弗朗索瓦·维勒鲁瓦·德加洛表示，欧洲央行不会仅基于能源价格来决定利率。【原文：The European Central Bank won’t decide on interest rates only on the basis of energy prices, Governing Council member Francois Villeroy de Galhau said.】

item.title: 拜尔斯道夫股价下跌，妮维雅制造商业绩预期疲软
item.description: 拜尔斯道夫公司预计本财年业绩将持续低迷，这家德国企业正努力重振其旗舰品牌妮维雅，同时应对护肤品市场增速放缓的挑战。【原文：Beiersdorf AG forecast a sluggish fiscal year as the German company struggles to revive its flagship Nivea brand and contends with a slowdown in the skincare market.】

item.title: 伊朗战争持续之际，亚洲争相寻求石油和天然气替代方案
item.description: 石油和天然气交易商近日忙于为亚洲市场寻找中东以外的替代供应来源，因担忧持续冲突可能导致这个能源富集地区的供应中断。【原文：Oil and gas traders have spent days scrambling for alternative sources of supply to Asia outside of the Middle East on concerns a prolonged conflict could choke off flows from the energy-rich region.】

item.title: 英国烘焙企业Greggs利润下滑，2026年初销售放缓
item.description: 英国连锁面包店Greggs Plc去年利润大幅下滑，因其正面临消费者信心疲软的挑战。【原文：Greggs Plc reported a slump in profit last year as the British high street bakery chain battles weak consumer sentiment.】

item.title: 瑞士央行言论升级，市场对瑞郎期权闪电交易保持警惕
item.description: 瑞士法郎的涨势可能正在失去动力，历史和图表都表明了这一点。在最近一轮强势上涨中，它遭遇了熟悉的障碍：瑞士国家银行。【原文：The Swiss franc’s rally may be running out of steam, history and charts suggest, after its latest burst of strength ran into a familiar obstacle: the Swiss National Bank.】

item.title: 英国食品通胀率2月小幅攀升，蒙蔽英格兰银行前景
item.description: 英国食品价格通胀率上月回升，因消费者为居家庆祝活动豪掷千金购买更昂贵的餐食套餐。【原文：Food price inflation in the UK rebounded last month as shoppers splashed out on pricier meal deals for at-home celebrations.】

item.title: 日本电产因会计丑闻将计提16亿美元费用
item.description: 日本电产公司表示，可能计提高达2500亿日元（约合16亿美元）的减值损失。作为全球最大的精密电机制造商，该公司正评估一起会计丑闻造成的损失，该丑闻导致其创始人与公司实质上断绝了关系。【原文：Nidec Corp. said it may book up to ¥250 billion ($1.6 billion) in impairment charges as the world’s largest manufacturer of precision motors looks to tally the cost of an accounting scandal that’s seen its founder effectively cut ties with the company.】

item.title: 南非股市处于甜蜜点，因黄金和兰特走强，美银表示
item.description: 美国银行表示，得益于金属价格飙升和兰特汇率走强，南非股市创纪录的12个月连涨行情仍将延续。【原文：South African stocks’ record 12-month winning streak has further to go thanks to booming metals prices and the firmer rand, according to Bank of America Corp.】

item.title: 欧洲天然气价格延续涨势，卡塔尔液化天然气供应中断引发市场担忧
item.description: 欧洲天然气价格连续第二日上涨，因卡塔尔全球最大液化天然气出口设施的停运将持续多久，以及对全球能源供应的影响仍存在不确定性。【原文：European natural gas prices rose for a second day amid uncertainty over how long exports will be halted from the world’s largest LNG export plant in Qatar and the impact it will have on global energy supplies.】

item.title: 丹麦大选前夕，奥斯特德公司面临新一轮撤资呼声
item.description: 据当地媒体报道，奥斯特德公司（Orsted A/S）再次成为丹麦政坛的热门话题，多家政党呼吁政府在即将举行的选举后出售其持有的这家风能公司的股份。【原文：Orsted A/S is again becoming a hot political topic in Denmark, with several parties calling on the government to sell its stake in the wind energy company after the upcoming election, according to local media.】

item.title: 野村证券前员工因入室抢劫案被判18年监禁
item.description: 据日本TBS电视台报道，一名被控纵火、谋杀未遂及抢劫老年客户的前野村控股公司员工，被日本法院判处18年监禁。【原文：A former Nomura Holdings Inc. employee who was accused of arson, attempted murder and robbing an elderly client was sentenced to 18 years in prison by a Japan court, local broadcaster TBS reported.】

item.title: 富时100指数将下跌，因伊朗战争引发股市抛售，英镑走低
亚洲炼油商或因霍尔木兹海峡拥堵降低加工率
item.description: 随着中东战争扩大以及霍尔木兹海峡航运受阻，亚洲炼油商正考虑降低产能利用率，以应对原油供应受阻的风险。【原文：Asian oil refiners are considering reducing operating rates as the widening Middle East war and difficulties shipping through the Strait of Hormuz threaten their access to crude.】

item.title: 俄罗斯央行就冻结国家资产起诉欧盟
item.description: 俄罗斯央行正就欧盟无限期冻结因普京总统入侵乌克兰而遭封锁的资产提起诉讼，称其被剥夺了法律保护。这是自战争爆发以来首次出现此类法律挑战。【原文：The Bank of Russia is suing the European Union for putting an indefinite freeze on assets blocked over President Vladimir Putin’s invasion of Ukraine, arguing that it’s being deprived of legal protections in a first challenge of its kind since the war erupted.】

item.title: 科技开发商Syzygy签署协议，旨在大幅提升可持续航空燃料供应量
item.description: 技术开发商Syzygy Plasmonics达成一系列初步协议，旨在美洲地区扩大低排放喷气燃料的生产规模。【原文：Technology developer Syzygy Plasmonics made a series of preliminary deals aimed at building out production of low-emission jet fuel across the Americas.】

item.title: Flink在交付量下滑后以更高估值完成融资
item.description: Flink SE在最新融资轮中估值达9亿美元，这表明这家生鲜配送初创企业已从疫情期间的估值峰值暴跌中稳定下来。【原文：Flink SE raised funds in a round that values the firm at $900 million, in a sign that the grocery delivery startup has stabilized after a plunge in its valuation from a pandemic-era peak.】

item.title: 欧洲经济可挺过伊朗战争——前提是战争一个月内结束
item.description: 未来四周将决定欧洲经济是面临新一轮危机，还是复苏进程中仅遭遇短暂的颠簸。【原文：The next four weeks will determine whether Europe’s economy is facing a fresh crisis or simply a speed bump in its recovery.】

item.title: 中国燃气买家称北京正敦促伊朗保持霍尔木兹海峡畅通
item.description: 天然气行业高管称，中国正施压伊朗官员避免采取可能干扰卡塔尔天然气出口或其他穿越霍尔木兹海峡（关键海上咽喉要道）能源运输的行动。【原文：Senior gas executives say China is pressuring Iranian officials to avoid action that would disrupt Qatari gas exports or other energy shipments making their way through the Strait of Hormuz, a key maritime chokepoint.】

item.title: 中国铜供应量激增，产量攀升至历史新高
item.description: 中国冶炼厂持续创纪录地增产铜，导致库存膨胀，并可能抑制这种金属强劲的涨势。【原文：Chinese smelters continue to churn out record quantities of copper, swelling stockpiles and threatening to slow the metal’s powerful gains.】

item.title: 日本邮政保险将转向高收益债券以应对利率上升
item.description: 日本邮政保险公司首席执行官表示，该公司计划抛售低收益政府债券，转而增持高收益债券，此举基于对进一步加息的预期。【原文：Japan Post Insurance Co. plans to sell holdings of lower-yielding government bonds and replace them with higher-yielding debt on expectations for further interest-rate hikes, according to its chief executive officer.】
